sections.heading: ERA Calculator: What It Is and How It Works
sections.content: The ERA Calculator is a free online tool that helps calculate a pitcher’s Earned Run Average (ERA) instantly using earned runs and innings pitched. ERA measures how many earned runs a pitcher allows per nine innings — a key pitching statistic.

subSections.heading: Enter Earned Runs
subSections.content: Input the total earned runs allowed by the pitcher.

subSections.heading: Enter Innings Pitched
subSections.content: Input the total innings pitched, including fractional innings (e.g., 6.1 = 6⅓ innings).

subSections.heading: Tip
subSections.content: Make sure inputs are accurate for precise results.

subSections.heading: Accurate & Reliable
subSections.content: Uses the standard formula: ERA = (Earned Runs ÷ Innings Pitched) × 9.

subSections.heading: Ideal for Fantasy Baseball & Analysis
subSections.content: Track pitcher performance easily for players, coaches, and analysts.

subSections.heading: Pitchers & Coaches
subSections.content: Track performance per game, series, or season.

subSections.heading: Fantasy Baseball
subSections.content: Evaluate pitcher value for drafts and scoring.

faq.a: It calculates a pitcher’s ERA using earned runs and innings pitched.
No, only earned runs are counted.

faq.q: Can I use it for fantasy baseball?
faq.a: Yes, it helps track pitcher stats quickly.
Players, coaches, fans, analysts, and fantasy enthusiasts.
Saves time and ensures accurate calculations.

faq.q: Can it track multiple games or seasons?
faq.a: Yes, using cumulative earned runs and innings pitched.
